topic_definition: Linguistic signals that a SWE job posting includes formal people-management responsibilities: having direct reports, leading/supervising a team, conducting 1:1s and performance reviews, owning headcount/hiring decisions, and managing career development. Distinct from purely technical leadership (architecture ownership) or informal mentorship, though boundaries are fuzzy and flagged below.

topic_exclusions.reason: Bare 'lead' is overwhelmingly a verb meaning 'drive/spearhead' (e.g., 'lead the design', 'lead initiatives', 'lead efforts') with no people-management content; also 'lead' the metal, 'lead time', 'lead generation'.
topic_exclusions.guard: Never match bare \blead\b. Always require multi-word forms ('team lead', 'tech lead', 'lead a team', 'lead engineer').

topic_exclusions.reason: Dominated by 'manage projects/stakeholders/expectations/risk/dependencies/your time' which are NOT people-management.
topic_exclusions.guard: Require object-level disambiguation: 'manage (the|a) team', 'manage engineers/developers/staff/people'. Do not match bare \bmanage\b.

topic_exclusions.reason: 'manager' appears in 'product manager', 'project manager', 'package manager', 'configuration manager', 'session manager', 'context manager' (Python).
topic_exclusions.guard: Require qualifier: 'engineering manager', 'people manager', 'line manager', 'hiring manager'. Do not match bare \bmanager\b.

topic_exclusions.reason: '1:1' literal also appears in 'aspect ratio 1:1', 'NAT 1:1', 'load balancer 1:1 mapping', timestamps like '1:10'.
topic_exclusions.guard: Use (?<!\d)1:1(?!\d) plus prefer collocation with 'meeting', 'with', 'weekly', 'cadence' for high precision; or accept some noise and rely on the compound-form variants ('one-on-one', 'one on one') as the cleaner signal.

topic_exclusions.reason: 'PR' = pull request, public relations, press release, Puerto Rico in location text, also a date prefix in 'PR-1234'.
topic_exclusions.guard: Don't include bare 'PR' in this vocabulary at all (it's not in our list, but the analyst should be aware nothing in this list expands to 'PR').

topic_exclusions.reason: 'PIP' = Python's pip package installer, very common in SWE JDs.
topic_exclusions.guard: Require 'PIP process', 'on a PIP', 'PIP plan', or full expansion 'performance improvement plan'.

topic_exclusions.reason: Generic 'reports' = 'status reports', 'bug reports', 'analytics reports', 'reporting dashboards', 'reporting tools'.
topic_exclusions.guard: Require multi-word forms with 'directly', 'to you', 'reporting line/structure/chain/hierarchy'. Never match bare \breport\b.

topic_exclusions.reason: 'head' verb/noun appears in 'head of partnerships', 'header', 'headers', 'overhead', 'heads-up display'.
topic_exclusions.guard: Match 'head of engineering', 'head the team', 'heads the team' as multi-word only.

topic_exclusions.reason: Dominated by technical 'performance optimization', 'system performance', 'high-performance', 'performance tuning', 'performance metrics'.
Two-letter 'EM' collides with 'EM dash', 'EMR', 'EMC', email-marketing acronyms, and is rarely written this way standalone in JDs.
topic_exclusions.guard: Require 'EM role' or 'as an EM' multi-word form. Prefer the spelled-out 'engineering manager'.

topic_exclusions.reason: 'IC' (individual contributor) is in this vocabulary's adjacent space but also collides with 'IC design' (integrated circuit), 'eIC', AWS 'IAM'.
topic_exclusions.guard: Not actually included in this list (IC is the OPPOSITE of people-mgmt), but if used elsewhere, require 'IC role', 'IC track', 'as an IC'.

topic_exclusions.reason: Mostly fine in SWE JDs but can appear as a noun in product names or unrelated.
Hits 'part of a team of engineers' (IC contexts) as well as 'manage a team of engineers'.
topic_exclusions.guard: Pair with 'manage/lead/oversee/head' verb in a 5-word window, or restrict to 'team of \d+' for headcount-quantification.

topic_calibration_recommendations: Spot-check these on a 100-post sample before trusting longitudinal trends: (1) 'Team leadership' — 'tech lead' / 'lead engineer' often denote senior-IC influence rather than formal management, and the IC-vs-manager mix may itself be shifting 2024->2026 (the very thing we want to measure), so distinguish formal-mgmt-lead from tech-lead in calibration; (2) '1:1 meetings' — verify the colon/hyphen variants are matching cleanly and that '1:1' isn't picking up technical ratios; (3) 'Headcount and hiring authority' — 'build the team' / 'grow the team' overlap with generic startup boilerplate; (4) 'Supervisory verbs' — confirm 'oversee' isn't dominated by 'oversee the architecture/delivery/migration' which is technical leadership not people supervision; (5) 'Coaching, mentoring, and career development' — staff/principal IC roles often claim 'mentor junior engineers' without people-mgmt authority, so this concept may over-count if used as a sole indicator.
topic_notes: Overlaps to coordinate with sibling vocabularies: (a) 'tech lead' / 'lead engineer' overlap with a Technical-leadership vocabulary if one exists — recommend they live there exclusively and we drop them here, OR be counted as a separate 'ambiguous-lead' bucket; current draft keeps them under Team leadership but flags it. (b) 'mentor/mentoring' overlaps with informal-mentorship vocab; current draft keeps it here as people-mgmt-adjacent. (c) Job-title parsing (Engineering Manager, Staff Engineer, etc.) is best done from the structured title field rather than description-keyword density — these markers measure JD-body language, which is a different signal. (d) The colon character in '1:1' breaks default \b…\b semantics; consumer should use the lookaround pattern recommended in regex_notes. (e) Recommend reporting per-1000-words density rather than per-posting hit rate, since posting length itself is shifting over the 2024-2026 window and would confound counts. (f) For longitudinal trend reliability, consider locking the keyword list at analysis start and treating any ad-hoc additions as a separate sensitivity check.
